sections.heading: Follow-Up
sections.content: After completion of therapy, follow-up should be coordinated with the primary care physician (PCP). A treatment summary including details of RT, OAR doses, and cumulative anthracycline dose should be provided. Follow-up with an oncologist is recommended due to risk of relapse and late effects [26]. Follow-up aims to: (1) Detect relapse early when potentially curative salvage therapy is possible; (2) Monitor for and manage long-term treatment effects (e.g., secondary malignancies, cardiovascular disease, endocrinopathies, infertility); (3) Provide survivorship counseling [26].

timeline.title: First 3-5 Years (Highest Relapse Risk)
timeline.body: Most relapses (80-90%) occur within the first 3-5 years after treatment [28]. Regular follow-up with an oncologist is recommended. Consider ECHO at 1-year post-anthracycline. Annual blood pressure monitoring and aggressive management of cardiovascular risk factors.

timeline.title: Long-Term Follow-Up (After 5 Years)
timeline.body: Lifelong surveillance for late effects. Consider ECHO at 5-10 year intervals. Consider carotid ultrasound at 10-year intervals if neck RT [26,27]. Annual breast screening (mammography + MRI) for individuals assigned female at birth with intact breast tissue who received mediastinal/axillary RT between ages 10-30 years, starting 8 years post-RT but not before age 25. Lung cancer screening per NCCN Lung Cancer Screening Guidelines for smokers/ex-smokers with thoracic RT [26]. Annual TSH if thyroid was in RT field [26]. Discuss family planning; evaluate for premature ovarian failure or gonadal dysfunction if indicated. Screen for distress, address survivorship concerns.

late_effects.name: Cardiovascular disease
late_effects.detail: Cardiovascular disease: Annual blood pressure, aggressive management of risk factors. Consider ECHO at 1-year post-anthracycline and then at 5-10 year intervals. Consider carotid US at 10-year intervals if neck RT [26,27].

late_effects.name: Secondary malignancies
late_effects.detail: Secondary malignancies: Annual breast screening (mammography + MRI) for individuals assigned female at birth with intact breast tissue who received mediastinal/axillary RT between ages 10-30 years, starting 8 years post-RT but not before age 25. Lung cancer screening per NCCN Lung Cancer Screening Guidelines for smokers/ex-smokers with thoracic RT [26].

late_effects.name: Thyroid dysfunction
late_effects.detail: Thyroid dysfunction: Annual TSH if thyroid was in RT field [26].

late_effects.name: Fertility
late_effects.detail: Fertility: Discuss family planning; evaluate for premature ovarian failure or gonadal dysfunction if indicated.

late_effects.name: Psychosocial
late_effects.detail: Psychosocial: Screen for distress, address survivorship concerns.
